description:
You are a mobile build-pipeline engineer double-checking that the
    container you are about to ship still resolves the most critical local
    host correctly.  
    Perform the following reliability check from the terminal:

    1. Make sure the directory 
       /home/user/build_checks
       exists.  
  
    2. Determine the primary IPv4 address that the system resolver returns
       for the hostname  
       localhost

       • Use whatever standard *command-line* resolver you prefer  
         (e.g. getent hosts, dig +short, host, etc.).  
       • Do **not** hard-code the address – actually ask the system.  

    3. Create the file
       /home/user/build_checks/dns_resolution.log  
       and write **exactly one line** to it whose format must be:

           <hostname>,<ip-address><newline>

       Concretely, after you are done the file’s entire, byte-for-byte
       contents must be:

           localhost,127.0.0.1\n

       where “\n” is a single UNIX line-feed (0x0a).  
       • There must be no extra spaces, no carriage return, and no blank
         lines before or after the line.  
       • The file must be UTF-8 encoded, plain text.  

    4. Leave the file in place; the automated test will read it to confirm
       that DNS resolution works as expected.  
       (Displaying the file to the console at the end is fine, but not
       strictly required.)

    Once you have finished, the grader will merely check that:

       - /home/user/build_checks/dns_resolution.log exists,
       - its sole line is *precisely* “localhost,127.0.0.1” followed by a
         single newline.

    You do not need root privileges for any of the above steps.
    A handful of common shell commands (mkdir, getent, echo, etc.) are more
    than enough—3–4 commands in total is typical.
truth:
The expected final state for verification:

    1. Directory:
       /home/user/build_checks
       • Must exist and be accessible (permissions 755 or more permissive).

    2. File:
       /home/user/build_checks/dns_resolution.log
       • Must exist.
       • Exact byte sequence (hex):
             6c 6f 63 61 6c 68 6f 73 74 2c 31 32 37 2e 30 2e 30 2e 31 0a
         i.e. ASCII/UTF-8 text:
             "localhost,127.0.0.1\n"
       • File size must therefore be exactly 20 bytes.

    No other files are required or will be inspected.
name: task_000000_d06c6b98
